name: South Korea
contextBlurb: As of September 2025, South Korea is moving toward a won-backed stablecoin regime but the final statutory/technical standards are pending. A KRW-pegged token (KRW1) has been launched in a pilot/PoC phase with bank-held reserves, while lawmakers and regulators finalize the framework that will govern issuance, reserves, and supervision.

legislation.title: KRW1 launched by BDACS on Avalanche with Woori Bank reserves – described as PoC/pilot
Reports indicate stablecoin bill to be submitted around October 2025 as part of Phase-2
DABA introduced to the National Assembly (ruling party initiative)

keyFigures.name: Financial Services Commission (FSC)
keyFigures.role: Policy lead and licensing/supervision for virtual-asset businesses under the new framework

keyFigures.name: Bank of Korea (BOK)
keyFigures.role: Central bank; advocates for strong role (even veto/backstop) given monetary/FX stability concerns

news.headline: Shin & Kim – Korea's emerging stablecoin framework
Financial Times – policy tug-of-war on stablecoins

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: As of September 2025, South Korea is moving toward a won-backed stablecoin regime but the final statutory/technical standards are pending. A KRW-pegged token (KRW1) has been launched in a pilot/PoC phase with bank-held reserves, while lawmakers and regulators finalize the framework that will govern issuance, reserves, and supervision.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Proposed classification under DABA distinguishes 'asset-linked digital assets' (including stablecoins) from other digital assets, with licensing by FSC and potential BOK role on stability issues. This is a bill, not yet settled law.

regulators.name: Financial Services Commission
regulators.role: Policy lead and licensing/supervision for virtual-asset businesses under the new framework

regulators.name: Bank of Korea
regulators.role: Central bank; advocates for strong role (even veto/backstop) given monetary/FX stability concerns
